special_rules: Each increase to Comforts & Necessities, Security, or Neighborhood costs 50¥ per level per month.
Assets and services must be usable or accessible by the general public and require gamemaster approval.
Street.description:
Street represents homelessness or an equally precarious existence. The character sleeps wherever shelter can be found, relies on scavenged or improvised necessities, and has almost no dependable privacy or protection.

Under the advanced lifestyle rules, Street begins with no ratings in Comforts & Necessities, Security, or Neighborhood. Increasing one of these categories costs a flat 50¥ per level per month. Assets and services must be publicly accessible and require gamemaster approval.

Squatter.description: Squatter provides a barely habitable place such as an abandoned building, crowded barracks, coffin motel, makeshift shack, or subdivided room. Food and utilities are poor, sanitation is unreliable, and meaningful security is uncommon.

Low.description: Low represents a basic apartment or similar modest residence. The character has regular meals, limited utilities, and a door that can usually be secured, though services may be rationed and local protection may depend on neighbourhood powers.

Middle.description: Middle provides a comfortable house, apartment, or condominium with dependable utilities, reasonable security, useful household technology, and a mixture of inexpensive and higher-quality food.

High.description: High provides a roomy home or condominium, good food, reliable domestic technology, housekeeping support, and strong neighbourhood security backed by respectable authorities, private security, or well-maintained local arrangements.

Luxury.description: Luxury offers premium residences, natural food and drink, extensive household staff or drones, sophisticated entertainment, elite social access, and top-level physical, Matrix, and astral security.

Bolt Hole.monthly_cost: 1,000¥ for each month in which it is used
special_rules: Increase the threshold of tests made to track a resident by 4.
Entertainment may contain assets, but not services or outings.
The full monthly cost is paid whenever the bolt hole is used during that month.
Bolt Hole.description:
Bolt Hole is a concealed refuge intended for hiding rather than comfortable daily living. It is isolated from people, the grid, and ordinary entertainment.

A resident increases the threshold of tests made to track them by 4. Only physical assets may be bought with Entertainment points; services and outings are unavailable. The full monthly cost is paid whenever the bolt hole is used during that month.

special_rules: Roll 1D6 + 2 each month to determine available lifestyle points.
The gamemaster determines the temporary premises and any available Entertainment.
Entertainment assets and services supplied by the establishment do not increase the lifestyle cost.
Traveler.description:
Traveler represents a mobile existence in temporary accommodation. The character stays somewhere different each month, ranging from discounted high-quality lodging to extremely poor short-term rooms.

At the beginning of each month, roll 1D6 + 2 to determine the available lifestyle points. The player allocates points to Comforts & Necessities, Security, or Neighborhood, while the gamemaster determines the premises and available Entertainment.

special_rules: This is a business location rather than a proper residence.
The premises include permits to sell selected legal items.
At the start of each month, make Charisma + Etiquette (Corporate) [Social]; each net hit reduces that month's cost by 1,000¥.
Commercial.description:
Commercial represents a storefront, workshop, or other business premises rather than a proper residence. It provides space to manufacture, repair, fence, or sell goods and includes permits for selected legal merchandise.

At the start of each month, a renting character makes a Charisma + Etiquette (Corporate) [Social] test. Each net hit reduces that month's cost by 1,000¥.

rules.Core Lifestyle Selection:
Core Lifestyle Selection
Every character selects a primary lifestyle representing their normal living standard. Additional residences may be maintained, but the full cost of every lifestyle must be paid.
rules.Advanced Lifestyle Categories:
Advanced Lifestyle Categories
Run Faster divides a lifestyle into Comforts & Necessities, Security, Neighborhood, and Entertainment. The first three use a base rating and maximum limit. Lifestyle points may raise these ratings or be spent on Entertainment; individual Entertainment extras are excluded from this file.
rules.Advanced Lifestyle Cost:
Advanced Lifestyle Cost
Except where a lifestyle states otherwise, each point spent to increase Comforts & Necessities, Security, or Neighborhood raises the monthly cost by 10% of the base lifestyle cost.

lifestyle_categories.Comforts & Necessities:
Comforts & Necessities
Represents shelter, rest, food, utilities, sanitation, and basic health needs. Under Run Faster, base lifestyle fatigue is 6S and each level reduces that Damage Value by 2.
lifestyle_categories.Security:
Security
Represents locks, surveillance, guards, privacy, local enforcement, and response to threats.
lifestyle_categories.Neighborhood:
Neighborhood
Represents the surrounding area's safety, legality, transport, environment, services, and social conditions.
lifestyle_categories.Entertainment:
Entertainment
Represents lifestyle-specific facilities, services, and outings. Individual extras are deliberately excluded.
